text.NRSV: In the third year of the reign of King Jehoiakim of Judah, King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came to Jerusalem and besieged it.
text.KJV: In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah came Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon unto Jerusalem, and besieged it.

text.NRSV: The Lord let King Jehoiakim of Judah fall into his power, as well as some of the vessels of the house of God. These he brought to the land of Shinar, and placed the vessels in the treasury of his gods.
text.KJV: And the Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand, with part of the vessels of the house of God: which he carried into the land of Shinar to the house of his god; and he brought the vessels into the treasure house of his god.

text.NRSV: Then the king commanded his palace master Ashpenaz to bring some of the Israelites of the royal family and of the nobility,
text.KJV: And the king spake unto Ashpenaz the master of his eunuchs, that he should bring certain of the children of Israel, and of the king’s seed, and of the princes;

text.NRSV: young men without physical defect and handsome, versed in every branch of wisdom, endowed with knowledge and insight, and competent to serve in the king’s palace; they were to be taught the literature and language of the Chaldeans.
text.KJV: Children in whom was no blemish, but well favoured, and skilful in all wisdom, and cunning in knowledge, and understanding science, and such as had ability in them to stand in the king’s palace, and whom they might teach the learning and the tongue of the Chaldeans.

text.NRSV: The king assigned them a daily portion of the royal rations of food and wine. They were to be educated for three years, so that at the end of that time they could be stationed in the king’s palace.
text.KJV: And the king appointed them a daily provision of the king’s meat, and of the wine which he drank: so nourishing them three years, that at the end thereof they might stand before the king.

text.NRSV: Among them were Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah, from the tribe of Judah.
text.KJV: Now among these were of the children of Judah, Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah:

text.NRSV: The palace master gave them other names: Daniel he called Belteshazzar, Hananiah he called Shadrach, Mishael he called Meshach, and Azariah he called Abednego.
text.KJV: Unto whom the prince of the eunuchs gave names: for he gave unto Daniel the name of Belteshazzar; and to Hananiah, of Shadrach; and to Mishael, of Meshach; and to Azariah, of Abed–nego.

text.NRSV: But Daniel resolved that he would not defile himself with the royal rations of food and wine; so he asked the palace master to allow him not to defile himself.
text.KJV: But Daniel purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the portion of the king’s meat, nor with the wine which he drank: therefore he requested of the prince of the eunuchs that he might not defile himself.

text.NRSV: Now God allowed Daniel to receive favor and compassion from the palace master.
text.KJV: Now God had brought Daniel into favour and tender love with the prince of the eunuchs.

text.NRSV: The palace master said to Daniel, “I am afraid of my lord the king; he has appointed your food and your drink. If he should see you in poorer condition than the other young men of your age, you would endanger my head with the king.”
text.KJV: And the prince of the eunuchs said unto Daniel, I fear my lord the king, who hath appointed your meat and your drink: for why should he see your faces worse liking than the children which are of your sort? then shall ye make me endanger my head to the king.

text.NRSV: Then Daniel asked the guard whom the palace master had appointed over Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah:
text.KJV: Then said Daniel to Melzar, whom the prince of the eunuchs had set over Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah,

text.NRSV: “Please test your servants for ten days. Let us be given vegetables to eat and water to drink.
text.KJV: Prove thy servants, I beseech thee, ten days; and let them give us pulse to eat, and water to drink.

text.NRSV: You can then compare our appearance with the appearance of the young men who eat the royal rations, and deal with your servants according to what you observe.”
text.KJV: Then let our countenances be looked upon before thee, and the countenance of the children that eat of the portion of the king’s meat: and as thou seest, deal with thy servants.

text.NRSV: So he agreed to this proposal and tested them for ten days.
text.KJV: So he consented to them in this matter, and proved them ten days.

text.NRSV: At the end of ten days it was observed that they appeared better and fatter than all the young men who had been eating the royal rations.
text.KJV: And at the end of ten days their countenances appeared fairer and fatter in flesh than all the children which did eat the portion of the king’s meat.

text.NRSV: So the guard continued to withdraw their royal rations and the wine they were to drink, and gave them vegetables.
text.KJV: Thus Melzar took away the portion of their meat, and the wine that they should drink; and gave them pulse.

text.NRSV: To these four young men God gave knowledge and skill in every aspect of literature and wisdom; Daniel also had insight into all visions and dreams.
text.KJV: As for these four children, God gave them knowledge and skill in all learning and wisdom: and Daniel had understanding in all visions and dreams.

text.NRSV: At the end of the time that the king had set for them to be brought in, the palace master brought them into the presence of Nebuchadnezzar,
text.KJV: Now at the end of the days that the king had said he should bring them in, then the prince of the eunuchs brought them in before Nebuchadnezzar.

text.NRSV: and the king spoke with them. And among them all, no one was found to compare with Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah; therefore they were stationed in the king’s palace.
text.KJV: And the king communed with them; and among them all was found none like Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah: therefore stood they before the king.

text.NRSV: In every matter of wisdom and understanding concerning which the king inquired of them, he found them ten times better than all the magicians and enchanters in his whole kingdom.
text.KJV: And in all matters of wisdom and understanding, that the king enquired of them, he found them ten times better than all the magicians and astrologers that were in all his realm.

text.NRSV: And Daniel continued there until the first year of King Cyrus.
text.KJV: And Daniel continued even unto the first year of king Cyrus.

text.NRSV: In the second year of as Nebuchadnezzar's reign, Nebuchadnezzar had dreams; his spirit was troubled and his sleep left him.
text.KJV: And in the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar Nebuchadnezzar dreamed dreams, wherewith his spirit was troubled, and his sleep brake from him.

text.NRSV: The king commanded that the magicians, the enchanters, the sorcerers, and the Chaldeans be summoned to tell the king his dreams. When they came in and stood before the king,
text.KJV: Then the king commanded to call the magicians, and the astrologers, and the sorcerers, and the Chaldeans, for to shew the king his dreams. So they came and stood before the king.

text.NRSV: he said to them, “I have had such a dream that my spirit is troubled by the desire to understand it.”
text.KJV: And the king said unto them, I have dreamed a dream, and my spirit was troubled to know the dream.

text.NRSV: The Chaldeans said to the king (in Aramaic), “O king, live forever! Tell your servants the dream, and we will reveal the interpretation.”
text.KJV: Then spake the Chaldeans to the king in Syriack, O king, live for ever: tell thy servants the dream, and we will shew the interpretation.

text.NRSV: The king answered the Chaldeans, “This is a public decree: if you do not tell me both the dream and its interpretation, you shall be torn limb from limb, and your houses shall be laid in ruins.
text.KJV: The king answered and said to the Chaldeans, The thing is gone from me: if ye will not make known unto me the dream, with the interpretation thereof, ye shall be cut in pieces, and your houses shall be made a dunghill.

text.NRSV: But if you do tell me the dream and its interpretation, you shall receive from me gifts and rewards and great honor. Therefore tell me the dream and its interpretation.”
text.KJV: But if ye shew the dream, and the interpretation thereof, ye shall receive of me gifts and rewards and great honour: therefore shew me the dream, and the interpretation thereof.

text.NRSV: They answered a second time, “Let the king tell his servants the dream, and we will reveal its interpretation.”
text.KJV: They answered again and said, Let the king tell his servants the dream, and we will shew the interpretation of it.

text.NRSV: The king answered, “I know with certainty that you are trying to gain time, because you see I have firmly decreed:
text.KJV: The king answered and said, I know of certainty that ye would gain the time, because ye see the thing is gone from me.

text.NRSV: if you do not tell me the dream, there is but one verdict for you. You have agreed to speak delusive and corrupt words to me until things take a change. Therefore tell me the dream, and I shall know that you can give me its interpretation.”
text.KJV: But if ye will not make known unto me the dream, there is but one decree for you: for ye have prepared lying and corrupt words to speak before me, till the time be changed: therefore tell me the dream, and I shall know that ye can shew me the interpretation thereof.

text.NRSV: The Chaldeans answered the king, “There is no one on earth who can satisfy the king’s demand, for no great and powerful king has ever asked such a thing of any magician or enchanter or Chaldean.
text.KJV: The Chaldeans answered before the king, and said, There is not a man upon the earth that can shew the king’s matter: therefore there is no king, lord, nor ruler, that asked such things at any magician, or astrologer, or Chaldean.

text.NRSV: The thing that the king is asking is too difficult, and no one can reveal it to the king except the gods, whose dwelling is not with mortals.”
text.KJV: And it is a rare thing that the king requireth, and there is none other that can shew it before the king, except the gods, whose dwelling is not with flesh.

text.NRSV: Because of this the king flew into a violent rage and commanded that all the wise men of Babylon be destroyed.
text.KJV: For this cause the king was angry and very furious, and commanded to destroy all the wise men of Babylon.

text.NRSV: The decree was issued, and the wise men were about to be executed; and they looked for Daniel and his companions, to execute them.
text.KJV: And the decree went forth that the wise men should be slain; and they sought Daniel and his fellows to be slain.

text.NRSV: Then Daniel responded with prudence and discretion to Arioch, the king’s chief executioner, who had gone out to execute the wise men of Babylon;
text.KJV: Then Daniel answered with counsel and wisdom to Arioch the captain of the king’s guard, which was gone forth to slay the wise men of Babylon:

text.NRSV: he asked Arioch, the royal officer, “Why is the decree of the king so urgent?” Arioch then explained the matter to Daniel.
text.KJV: He answered and said to Arioch the king’s captain, Why is the decree so hasty from the king? Then Arioch made the thing known to Daniel.

text.NRSV: So Daniel went in and requested that the king give him time and he would tell the king the interpretation.
text.KJV: Then Daniel went in, and desired of the king that he would give him time, and that he would shew the king the interpretation.

text.NRSV: Then Daniel went to his home and informed his companions, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah,
text.KJV: Then Daniel went to his house, and made the thing known to Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah, his companions:

text.NRSV: and told them to seek mercy from the God of heaven concerning this mystery, so that Daniel and his companions might not perish with the rest of the wise men of Babylon.
text.KJV: That they would desire mercies of the God of heaven concerning this secret; that Daniel and his fellows should not perish with the rest of the wise men of Babylon.

text.NRSV: Then the mystery was revealed to Daniel in a vision of the night, and Daniel blessed the God of heaven.
text.KJV: Then was the secret revealed unto Daniel in a night vision. Then Daniel blessed the God of heaven.

text.NRSV: Daniel said: “Blessed be the name of God from age to age, for wisdom and power are his.
text.KJV: Daniel answered and said, Blessed be the name of God for ever and ever: for wisdom and might are his:

text.NRSV: He changes times and seasons, deposes kings and sets up kings; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding;
text.KJV: And he changeth the times and the seasons: he removeth kings, and setteth up kings: he giveth wisdom unto the wise, and knowledge to them that know understanding:

text.NRSV: he reveals deep and hidden things; he knows what is in the darkness, and light dwells with him.
text.KJV: He revealeth the deep and secret things: he knoweth what is in the darkness, and the light dwelleth with him.

text.NRSV: To you, O God of my ancestors, I give thanks and praise, for you have given me wisdom and power, and have now revealed to me what we asked of you, for you have revealed to us what the king ordered.”
text.KJV: I thank thee, and praise thee, O thou God of my fathers, who hast given me wisdom and might, and hast made known unto me now what we desired of thee: for thou hast now made known unto us the king’s matter.

text.NRSV: Therefore Daniel went to Arioch, whom the king had appointed to destroy the wise men of Babylon, and said to him: “Do not destroy the wise men of Babylon; bring me in before the king, and I will give the king the interpretation.”
text.KJV: Therefore Daniel went in unto Arioch, whom the king had ordained to destroy the wise men of Babylon: he went and said thus unto him; Destroy not the wise men of Babylon: bring me in before the king, and I will shew unto the king the interpretation.

text.NRSV: Then Arioch quickly brought Daniel before the king and said to him: “I have found among the exiles from Judah a man who can tell the king the interpretation.”
text.KJV: Then Arioch brought in Daniel before the king in haste, and said thus unto him, I have found a man of the captives of Judah, that will make known unto the king the interpretation.

text.NRSV: The king said to Daniel, whose name was Belteshazzar, “Are you able to tell me the dream that I have seen and its interpretation?”
text.KJV: The king answered and said to Daniel, whose name was Belteshazzar, Art thou able to make known unto me the dream which I have seen, and the interpretation thereof?

text.NRSV: Daniel answered the king, “No wise men, enchanters, magicians, or diviners can show to the king the mystery that the king is asking,
text.KJV: Daniel answered in the presence of the king, and said, The secret which the king hath demanded cannot the wise men, the astrologers, the magicians, the soothsayers, shew unto the king;

text.NRSV: but there is a God in heaven who reveals mysteries, and he has disclosed to King Nebuchadnezzar what will happen at the end of days. Your dream and the visions of your head as you lay in bed were these:
text.KJV: But there is a God in heaven that revealeth secrets, and maketh known to the king Nebuchadnezzar what shall be in the latter days. Thy dream, and the visions of thy head upon thy bed, are these;

text.NRSV: To you, O king, as you lay in bed, came thoughts of what would be hereafter, and the revealer of mysteries disclosed to you what is to be.
text.KJV: As for thee, O king, thy thoughts came into thy mind upon thy bed, what should come to pass hereafter: and he that revealeth secrets maketh known to thee what shall come to pass.

text.NRSV: But as for me, this mystery has not been revealed to me because of any wisdom that I have more than any other living being, but in order that the interpretation may be made known to the king and that you may understand the thoughts of your mind.
text.KJV: But as for me, this secret is not revealed to me for any wisdom that I have more than any living, but for their sakes that shall make known the interpretation to the king, and that thou mightest know the thoughts of thy heart.

text.NRSV: “You were looking, O king, and lo! there was a great statue. This statue was huge, its brilliance extraordinary; it was standing before you, and its appearance was frightening.
text.KJV: Thou, O king, sawest, and behold a great image. This great image, whose brightness was excellent, stood before thee; and the form thereof was terrible.

text.NRSV: The head of that statue was of fine gold, its chest and arms of silver, its midsection and thighs of bronze,
text.KJV: This image’s head was of fine gold, his breast and his arms of silver, his belly and his thighs of brass,

text.NRSV: its legs of iron, its feet partly of iron and partly of clay.
text.KJV: His legs of iron, his feet part of iron and part of clay.

text.NRSV: As you looked on, a stone was cut out, not by human hands, and it struck the statue on its feet of iron and clay and broke them in pieces.
text.KJV: Thou sawest till that a stone was cut out without hands, which smote the image upon his feet that were of iron and clay, and brake them to pieces.

text.NRSV: Then the iron, the clay, the bronze, the silver, and the gold were all broken in pieces and became like the chaff of the summer threshing floors; and the wind carried them away, so that not a trace of them could be found. But the stone that struck the statue became a great mountain and filled the whole earth.
text.KJV: Then was the iron, the clay, the brass, the silver, and the gold, broken to pieces together, and became like the chaff of the summer threshingfloors; and the wind carried them away, that no place was found for them: and the stone that smote the image became a great mountain, and filled the whole earth.

text.NRSV: “This was the dream; now we will tell the king its interpretation.
text.KJV: This is the dream; and we will tell the interpretation thereof before the king.

text.NRSV: You, O king, the king of kings—to whom the God of heaven has given the kingdom, the power, the might, and the glory,
text.KJV: Thou, O king, art a king of kings: for the God of heaven hath given thee a kingdom, power, and strength, and glory.

text.NRSV: into whose hand he has given human beings, wherever they live, the wild animals of the field, and the birds of the air, and whom he has established as ruler over them all—you are the head of gold.
text.KJV: And wheresoever the children of men dwell, the beasts of the field and the fowls of the heaven hath he given into thine hand, and hath made thee ruler over them all. Thou art this head of gold.

text.NRSV: After you shall arise another kingdom inferior to yours, and yet a third kingdom of bronze, which shall rule over the whole earth.
text.KJV: And after thee shall arise another kingdom inferior to thee, and another third kingdom of brass, which shall bear rule over all the earth.

text.NRSV: And there shall be a fourth kingdom, strong as iron; just as iron crushes and smashes everything, it shall crush and shatter all these.
text.KJV: And the fourth kingdom shall be strong as iron: forasmuch as iron breaketh in pieces and subdueth all things: and as iron that breaketh all these, shall it break in pieces and bruise.

text.NRSV: As you saw the feet and toes partly of potter’s clay and partly of iron, it shall be a divided kingdom; but some of the strength of iron shall be in it, as you saw the iron mixed with the clay.
text.KJV: And whereas thou sawest the feet and toes, part of potters’ clay, and part of iron, the kingdom shall be divided; but there shall be in it of the strength of the iron, forasmuch as thou sawest the iron mixed with miry clay.

text.NRSV: As the toes of the feet were part iron and part clay, so the kingdom shall be partly strong and partly brittle.
text.KJV: And as the toes of the feet were part of iron, and part of clay, so the kingdom shall be partly strong, and partly broken.

text.NRSV: As you saw the iron mixed with clay, so will they mix with one another in marriage, but they will not hold together, just as iron does not mix with clay.
text.KJV: And whereas thou sawest iron mixed with miry clay, they shall mingle themselves with the seed of men: but they shall not cleave one to another, even as iron is not mixed with clay.

text.NRSV: And in the days of those kings the God of heaven will set up a kingdom that shall never be destroyed, nor shall this kingdom be left to another people. It shall crush all these kingdoms and bring them to an end, and it shall stand forever;
text.KJV: And in the days of these kings shall the God of heaven set up a kingdom, which shall never be destroyed: and the kingdom shall not be left to other people, but it shall break in pieces and consume all these kingdoms, and it shall stand for ever.

text.NRSV: just as you saw that a stone was cut from the mountain not by hands, and that it crushed the iron, the bronze, the clay, the silver, and the gold. The great God has informed the king what shall be hereafter. The dream is certain, and its interpretation trustworthy.”
text.KJV: Forasmuch as thou sawest that the stone was cut out of the mountain without hands, and that it brake in pieces the iron, the brass, the clay, the silver, and the gold; the great God hath made known to the king what shall come to pass hereafter: and the dream is certain, and the interpretation thereof sure.

text.NRSV: Then King Nebuchadnezzar fell on his face, worshiped Daniel, and commanded that a grain offering and incense be offered to him.
text.KJV: Then the king Nebuchadnezzar fell upon his face, and worshipped Daniel, and commanded that they should offer an oblation and sweet odours unto him.

text.NRSV: The king said to Daniel, “Truly, your God is God of gods and Lord of kings and a revealer of mysteries, for you have been able to reveal this mystery!”
text.KJV: The king answered unto Daniel, and said, Of a truth it is, that your God is a God of gods, and a Lord of kings, and a revealer of secrets, seeing thou couldest reveal this secret.

text.NRSV: Then the king promoted Daniel, gave him many great gifts, and made him ruler over the whole province of Babylon and chief prefect over all the wise men of Babylon.
text.KJV: Then the king made Daniel a great man, and gave him many great gifts, and made him ruler over the whole province of Babylon, and chief of the governors over all the wise men of Babylon.

text.NRSV: At Daniel’s request the king appointed Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego over the affairs of the province of Babylon. But Daniel remained at the king’s court.
text.KJV: Then Daniel requested of the king, and he set Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, over the affairs of the province of Babylon: but Daniel sat in the gate of the king.

text.NRSV: King Nebuchadnezzar made a golden statue whose height was sixty cubits and whose width was six cubits; he set it up on the plain of Dura in the province of Babylon.
text.KJV: Nebuchadnezzar the king made an image of gold, whose height was threescore cubits, and the breadth thereof six cubits: he set it up in the plain of Dura, in the province of Babylon.

text.NRSV: Then King Nebuchadnezzar sent for the satraps, the prefects, and the governors, the counselors, the treasurers, the justices, the magistrates, and all the officials of the provinces to assemble and come to the dedication of the statue that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up.
text.KJV: Then Nebuchadnezzar the king sent to gather together the princes, the governors, and the captains, the judges, the treasurers, the counsellors, the sheriffs, and all the rulers of the provinces, to come to the dedication of the image which Nebuchadnezzar the king had set up.

text.NRSV: So the satraps, the prefects, and the governors, the counselors, the treasurers, the justices, the magistrates, and all the officials of the provinces, assembled for the dedication of the statue that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up. When they were standing before the statue that Nebuchadnezzar had set up,
text.KJV: Then the princes, the governors, and captains, the judges, the treasurers, the counsellors, the sheriffs, and all the rulers of the provinces, were gathered together unto the dedication of the image that Nebuchadnezzar the king had set up; and they stood before the image that Nebuchadnezzar had set up.

text.NRSV: the herald proclaimed aloud, “You are commanded, O peoples, nations, and languages,
text.KJV: Then an herald cried aloud, To you it is commanded, O people, nations, and languages,

text.NRSV: that when you hear the sound of the horn, pipe, lyre, trigon, harp, drum, and entire musical ensemble, you are to fall down and worship the golden statue that King Nebuchadnezzar has set up.
text.KJV: That at what time ye hear the sound of the cornet, flute, harp, sackbut, psaltery, dulcimer, and all kinds of musick, ye fall down and worship the golden image that Nebuchadnezzar the king hath set up:

text.NRSV: Whoever does not fall down and worship shall immediately be thrown into a furnace of blazing fire.”
text.KJV: And whoso falleth not down and worshippeth shall the same hour be cast into the midst of a burning fiery furnace.

text.NRSV: Therefore, as soon as all the peoples heard the sound of the horn, pipe, lyre, trigon, harp, drum, and entire musical ensemble, all the peoples, nations, and languages fell down and worshiped the golden statue that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up.
text.KJV: Therefore at that time, when all the people heard the sound of the cornet, flute, harp, sackbut, psaltery, and all kinds of musick, all the people, the nations, and the languages, fell down and worshipped the golden image that Nebuchadnezzar the king had set up.

text.NRSV: Accordingly, at this time certain Chaldeans came forward and denounced the Jews.
text.KJV: Wherefore at that time certain Chaldeans came near, and accused the Jews.

text.NRSV: They said to King Nebuchadnezzar, “O king, live forever!
text.KJV: They spake and said to the king Nebuchadnezzar, O king, live for ever.

text.NRSV: You, O king, have made a decree, that everyone who hears the sound of the horn, pipe, lyre, trigon, harp, drum, and entire musical ensemble, shall fall down and worship the golden statue,
text.KJV: Thou, O king, hast made a decree, that every man that shall hear the sound of the cornet, flute, harp, sackbut, psaltery, and dulcimer, and all kinds of musick, shall fall down and worship the golden image:

text.NRSV: and whoever does not fall down and worship shall be thrown into a furnace of blazing fire.
text.KJV: And whoso falleth not down and worshippeth, that he should be cast into the midst of a burning fiery furnace.

text.NRSV: There are certain Jews whom you have appointed over the affairs of the province of Babylon: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego. These pay no heed to you, O king. They do not serve your gods and they do not worship the golden statue that you have set up.”
text.KJV: There are certain Jews whom thou hast set over the affairs of the province of Babylon, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego; these men, O king, have not regarded thee: they serve not thy gods, nor worship the golden image which thou hast set up.

text.NRSV: Then Nebuchadnezzar in furious rage commanded that Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego be brought in; so they brought those men before the king.
text.KJV: Then Nebuchadnezzar in his rage and fury commanded to bring Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego. Then they brought these men before the king.

text.NRSV: Nebuchadnezzar said to them, “Is it true, O Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, that you do not serve my gods and you do not worship the golden statue that I have set up?
text.KJV: Nebuchadnezzar spake and said unto them, Is it true, O Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, do not ye serve my gods, nor worship the golden image which I have set up?

text.NRSV: Now if you are ready when you hear the sound of the horn, pipe, lyre, trigon, harp, drum, and entire musical ensemble to fall down and worship the statue that I have made, well and good. But if you do not worship, you shall immediately be thrown into a furnace of blazing fire, and who is the god that will deliver you out of my hands?”
text.KJV: Now if ye be ready that at what time ye hear the sound of the cornet, flute, harp, sackbut, psaltery, and dulcimer, and all kinds of musick, ye fall down and worship the image which I have made; well: but if ye worship not, ye shall be cast the same hour into the midst of a burning fiery furnace; and who is that God that shall deliver you out of my hands?

text.NRSV: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego answered the king, “O Nebuchadnezzar, we have no need to present a defense to you in this matter.
text.KJV: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, answered and said to the king, O Nebuchadnezzar, we are not careful to answer thee in this matter.

text.NRSV: If our God whom we serve is able to deliver us from the furnace of blazing fire and out of your hand, O king, let him deliver us.
text.KJV: If it be so, our God whom we serve is able to deliver us from the burning fiery furnace, and he will deliver us out of thine hand, O king.

text.NRSV: But if not, be it known to you, O king, that we will not serve your gods and we will not worship the golden statue that you have set up.”
text.KJV: But if not, be it known unto thee, O king, that we will not serve thy gods, nor worship the golden image which thou hast set up.

text.NRSV: Then Nebuchadnezzar was so filled with rage against Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego that his face was distorted. He ordered the furnace heated seven times a more than was customary,
text.KJV: Then was Nebuchadnezzar full of fury, and the form of his visage was changed against Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego: therefore he spake, and commanded that they should heat the furnace one seven times more than it was wont to be heated.

text.NRSV: and ordered some of the strongest guards in his army to bind Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego and to throw them into the furnace of blazing fire.
text.KJV: And he commanded the most mighty men that were in his army to bind Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, and to cast them into the burning fiery furnace.

text.NRSV: So the men were bound, still wearing their tunics, their trousers, their hats, and their other garments, and they were thrown into the furnace of blazing fire.
text.KJV: Then these men were bound in their coats, their hosen, and their hats, and their other garments, and were cast into the midst of the burning fiery furnace.

text.NRSV: Because the king’s command was urgent and the furnace was so overheated, the raging flames killed the men who lifted Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego.
text.KJV: Therefore because the king’s commandment was urgent, and the furnace exceeding hot, the flame of the fire slew those men that took up Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego.

text.NRSV: But the three men, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, fell down, bound, into the furnace of blazing fire.
text.KJV: And these three men, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, fell down bound into the midst of the burning fiery furnace.

text.NRSV: They walked around in the midst of the flames, singing hymns to God and blessing the Lord.
text.KJV: And they walked in the midst of the fire, praising God, and blessing the Lord.
text.NABRE: They walked about in the flames, singing to God and blessing the Lord.
text.DRB: And they walked in the midst of the flame praising God, and blessing our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Azariah stood still in the midst of the fire and prayed aloud:
text.KJV: Then Azarias stood up, and prayed on this manner; and opening his mouth in the midst of the fire said,
text.NABRE: Azariah stood up in the midst of the fire and prayed aloud:
text.DRB: And Azarias standing prayed thus, and opening his mouth in the midst of the fire, he said:

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you, O Lord, God of our ancestors, and worthy of praise; and glorious is your name forever!”
text.KJV: Blessed art thou, O Lord God of our fathers: thy name is worthy to be praised and glorified for evermore:
text.NABRE: “Blessed are you, and praiseworthy, O Lord, the God of our ancestors, and glorious forever is your name.
text.DRB: Blessed art thou o Lord the God of our fathers, and laudable, and glorious is thy name for ever:

text.NRSV: “For you are just in all you have done; all your works are true and your ways right, and all your judgments are true.”
text.KJV: For thou art righteous in all the things that thou hast done to us: yea, true are all thy works, thy ways are right, and all thy judgments truth.
text.NABRE: For you are just in all you have done; all your deeds are faultless, all your ways right, and all your judgments proper.
text.DRB: because thou art just in all things, which thou hast done to us, and all thy works are true, and thy ways right, and all thy judgements true.

text.NRSV: “You have executed true judgments in all that you have brought upon us and upon Jerusalem, the holy city of our ancestors; for in truth and justice you have brought all this upon us because of our sins.”
text.KJV: In all the things that thou hast brought upon us, and upon the holy city of our fathers, even Jerusalem, thou hast executed true judgment: for according to truth and judgment didst thou bring all these things upon us because of our sins.
text.NABRE: You have executed proper judgments in all that you have brought upon us and upon Jerusalem, the holy city of our ancestors. By a proper judgment you have done all this because of our sins;
text.DRB: For thou hast done true judgements according to all things, that thou hast brought upon us, and upon the holy city of our fathers Jerusalem: because in truth, & in judgement thou hast brought in all these things for our sins.

text.NRSV: “For we have sinfully and lawlessly departed from you, and have sinned in all things and have not obeyed your commandments;”
text.KJV: For we have sinned and committed iniquity, departing from thee.
text.NABRE: For we have sinned and transgressed by departing from you, and we have done every kind of evil.
text.DRB: For we have sinned, and done unjustly revolting from thee: and we have offended in all things;

text.NRSV: “we have not observed them or done as you have commanded us, so that it might go well with us.”
text.KJV: In all things have we trespassed, and not obeyed thy commandments, nor kept them, neither done as thou hast commanded us, that it might go well with us.
text.NABRE: Your commandments we have not heeded or observed, nor have we done as you ordered us for our good.
text.DRB: and thy precepts we have not heard, nor observed, nor done as thou hadst commanded us, that it might be well with us.

text.NRSV: “So all that you have brought upon us, and all that you have done to us, you have done by a true judgment.”
text.KJV: Wherefore all that thou hast brought upon us, and every thing that thou hast done to us, thou hast done in true judgment.
text.NABRE: Therefore all you have brought upon us, all you have done to us, you have done by a proper judgment.
text.DRB: All things therefore that thou hast brought in upon us, and all things that thou hast done to us, thou hast done with true judgement:

text.NRSV: “You have handed us over to our enemies, lawless and hateful rebels, and to an unjust king, the most wicked in all the world.”
text.KJV: And thou didst deliver us into the hands of lawless enemies, most hateful forsakers of God, and to an unjust king, and the most wicked in all the world.
text.NABRE: You have handed us over to our enemies, lawless and hateful rebels; to an unjust king, the worst in all the world.
text.DRB: and thou hast delivered us into the hands of our enemies unjust, & most wicked, & prevaricators, & to an unjust king and most wicked above all the earth.

text.NRSV: “And now we cannot open our mouths; we, your servants who worship you, have become a shame and a reproach.”
text.KJV: And now we cannot open our mouths, we are become a shame and reproach to thy servants; and to them that worship thee.
text.NABRE: Now we cannot open our mouths; shame and reproach have come upon us, your servants, who revere you.
text.DRB: And now we cannot open the mouth: we are become a confusion, and reproach to thy servants, & to them that worship thee.

text.NRSV: “For your name’s sake do not give us up forever, and do not annul your covenant.”
text.KJV: Yet deliver us not up wholly, for thy name’s sake, neither disannul thou thy covenant:
text.NABRE: For your name’s sake, do not deliver us up forever, or make void your covenant.
text.DRB: Deliver us not for ever, we beseech thee, for thy name sake, and dissipate not thy testament:

text.NRSV: “Do not withdraw your mercy from us, for the sake of Abraham your beloved and for the sake of Isaac your servant and Israel your holy one,”
text.KJV: And cause not thy mercy to depart from us, for thy beloved Abraham’s sake, for thy servant Issac’s sake, and for thy holy Israel’s sake;
text.NABRE: Do not take away your mercy from us, for the sake of Abraham, your beloved, Isaac your servant, and Israel your holy one,
text.DRB: neither take thou away thy mercy from us, for Abraham thy beloved, and Isaac thy servant, and Israel thy holy one:

text.NRSV: “to whom you promised to multiply their descendants like the stars of heaven and like the sand on the shore of the sea.”
text.KJV: To whom thou hast spoken and promised, that thou wouldest multiply their seed as the stars of heaven, and as the sand that lieth upon the seashore.
text.NABRE: To whom you promised to multiply their offspring like the stars of heaven, or the sand on the shore of the sea.
text.DRB: to whom thou hast spoken, promising that thou wouldest multiply their seed as the stars of heaven, and as the sand that is in the sea shore.

text.NRSV: “For we, O Lord, have become fewer than any other nation, and are brought low this day in all the world because of our sins.”
text.KJV: For we, O Lord, are become less than any nation, and be kept under this day in all the world because of our sins.
text.NABRE: For we are reduced, O Lord, beyond any other nation, brought low everywhere in the world this day because of our sins.
text.DRB: Because o Lord we are diminished more than all nations, and are abased in all the earth this day for our sins.

text.NRSV: “In our day we have no ruler, or prophet, or leader, no burnt offering, or sacrifice, or oblation, or incense, no place to make an offering before you and to find mercy.”
text.KJV: Neither is there at this time prince, or prophet, or leader, or burnt offering, or sacrifice, or oblation, or incense, or place to sacrifice before thee, and to find mercy.
text.NABRE: We have in our day no prince, prophet, or leader, no burnt offering, sacrifice, oblation, or incense, no place to offer first fruits, to find favor with you.
text.DRB: And there is not at this time prince, & duke, and prophet, nor holocaust, nor sacrifice, nor oblation, nor incense, nor place of first fruits before thee,

text.NRSV: “Yet with a contrite heart and a humble spirit may we be accepted,”
text.KJV: Nevertheless in a contrite heart and an humble spirit let us be accepted.
text.NABRE: But with contrite heart and humble spirit let us be received; As though it were burnt offerings of rams and bulls, or tens of thousands of fat lambs,
text.DRB: that we may find thy mercy: but in contrite mind, & spirit of humility let us be received.

text.NRSV: “as though it were with burnt offerings of rams and bulls, or with tens of thousands of fat lambs; such may our sacrifice be in your sight this day, and may we unreservedly follow you, for no shame will come to those who trust in you.”
text.KJV: Like as in the burnt offerings of rams and bullocks, and like as in ten thousands of fat lambs: so let our sacrifice be in thy sight this day, and grant that we may wholly go after thee: for they shall not be confounded that put their trust in thee.
text.NABRE: So let our sacrifice be in your presence today and find favor before you; for those who trust in you cannot be put to shame.
text.DRB: As in holocaust of rams, and bulls, and as in thousands of fat lambs: so let our sacrifice be made in thy sight this day, that it may please thee: because there is no confusion to them that trust in thee.

text.NRSV: “And now with all our heart we follow you; we fear you and seek your presence. Do not put us to shame,”
text.KJV: And now we follow thee with all our heart, we fear thee, and seek thy face.
text.NABRE: And now we follow you with our whole heart, we fear you and we seek your face. Do not put us to shame,
text.DRB: And now we follow thee in all our heart, and fear thee, and seek thy face.

text.NRSV: “but deal with us in your forbearance and in your abundant mercy.”
text.KJV: Put us not to shame: but deal with us after thy lovingkindness, and according to the multitude of thy mercies.
text.NABRE: but deal with us in your kindness and great mercy.
text.DRB: Confound us not, but do with us according to thy meekness, and according to the multitude of thy mercy.

text.NRSV: “Deliver us in accordance with your marvelous works, and bring glory to your name, O Lord.”
text.KJV: Deliver us also according to thy marvellous works, and give glory to thy name, O Lord: and let all them that do thy servants hurt be ashamed;
text.NABRE: Deliver us in accord with your wonders, and bring glory to your name, O Lord:
text.DRB: And deliver us in thy marvels, and give glory to thy name o Lord:

text.NRSV: “Let all who do harm to your servants be put to shame; let them be disgraced and deprived of all power, and let their strength be broken.”
text.KJV: And let them be confounded in all their power and might, and let their strength be broken;
text.NABRE: Let all those be put to shame who inflict evils on your servants; Let them be shamed and powerless, and their strength broken;
text.DRB: and let all be confounded that shew evils to thy servants, let them be confounded in all thy might, and let their strength be broken:

text.NRSV: “Let them know that you alone are the Lord God, glorious over the whole world.”
text.KJV: And let them know that thou art God, the only God, and glorious over the whole world.
text.NABRE: Let them know that you alone are the Lord God, glorious over the whole world.”
text.DRB: and let them know that thou art the Lord, the only God, and glorious over the round world.

text.NRSV: Now the king’s servants who threw them in kept stoking the furnace with naphtha, pitch, tow, and brushwood.
text.KJV: And the king’s servants, that put them in, ceased not to make the oven hot with rosin, pitch, tow, and small wood;
text.NABRE: Now the king’s servants who had thrown them in continued to stoke the furnace with naphtha, pitch, tow, and brush.
text.DRB: And the king's servants that had cast them in, ceased not to heat the furnace, with Naphtha, and tow, and pitch, and dry sticks,

text.NRSV: And the flames poured out above the furnace forty-nine cubits,
text.KJV: So that the flame streamed forth above the furnace forty and nine cubits.
text.NABRE: The flames rose forty-nine cubits above the furnace,
text.DRB: and the flame mounted out above the furnace nine and forty cubits:

text.NRSV: and spread out and burned those Chaldeans who were caught near the furnace.
text.KJV: And it passed through, and burned those Chaldeans it found about the furnace.
text.NABRE: and spread out, burning the Chaldeans that it caught around the furnace.
text.DRB: and it brake forth, and burnt them whom it found by the furnace, of the Chaldees.

text.NRSV: But the angel of the Lord came down into the furnace to be with Azariah and his companions, and drove the fiery flame out of the furnace,
text.KJV: But the angel of the Lord came down into the oven together with Azarias and his fellows, and smote the flame of the fire out of the oven;
text.NABRE: But the angel of the Lord went down into the furnace with Azariah and his companions, drove the fiery flames out of the furnace,
text.DRB: But the Angel of our Lord descended with Azarias, and his fellows into the furnace: and he shook the flame of the fire out of the furnace,

text.NRSV: and made the inside of the furnace as though a moist, whistling wind were passing through it. The fire did not touch them at all and caused them no pain or distress.
text.KJV: And made the midst of the furnace as it had been a moist whistling wind, so that the fire touched them not at all, neither hurt nor troubled them.
text.NABRE: and made the inside of the furnace as though a dew-laden breeze were blowing through it. The fire in no way touched them or caused them pain or harm.
text.DRB: and made the midst of the furnace as a wind of dew blowing, and the fire touched them not at all, nor pained them, nor did them any grievance.

text.NRSV: Then the three with one voice praised and glorified and blessed God in the furnace, saying:
text.KJV: Then the three, as out of one mouth, praised, glorified, and blessed, God in the furnace, saying,
text.NABRE: Then these three in the furnace with one voice sang, glorifying and blessing God:
text.DRB: Then these three as out of one mouth praised, and glorified, and blessed God in the furnace, saying:

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you, Lord God of our ancestors, and to be praised and highly exalted forever; And blessed is your glorious, holy name, and to be highly praised and highly exalted forever.”
text.KJV: Blessed art thou, O Lord God of our fathers: and to be praised and exalted above all for ever. And blessed is thy glorious and holy name: and to be praised and exalted above all for ever.
text.NABRE: “Blessed are you, O Lord, the God of our ancestors, praiseworthy and exalted above all forever; And blessed is your holy and glorious name, praiseworthy and exalted above all for all ages.
text.DRB: Blessed art thou o Lord the God of our fathers: and laudable, and glorious, and superexalted for ever: and blessed is the holy name of thy glory: and laudable, and superexalted in all ages.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you in the temple of your holy glory, and to be extolled and highly glorified forever.”
text.KJV: Blessed art thou in the temple of thine holy glory: and to be praised and glorified above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Blessed are you in the temple of your holy glory, praiseworthy and glorious above all forever.
text.DRB: Blessed art thou in the holy temple of thy glory: & passing laudable, and passing glorious for ever.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you who look into the depths from your throne on the cherubim, and to be praised and highly exalted forever.”
text.KJV: Blessed art thou that beholdest the depths, and sittest upon the cherubims: and to be praised and exalted above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Blessed are you on the throne of your kingdom, praiseworthy and exalted above all forever.
text.DRB: Blessed art thou in the throne of thy kingdom, and passing laudable, and superexalted for ever.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you on the throne of your kingdom, and to be extolled and highly exalted forever.”
text.KJV: Blessed art thou on the glorious throne of thy kingdom: and to be praised and glorified above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Blessed are you who look into the depths from your throne upon the cherubim, praiseworthy and exalted above all forever.
text.DRB: Blessed art thou, that beholdest the depths, and sittest upon the Cherubs: and laudable, and superexalted for ever.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you in the firmament of heaven, and to be sung and glorified forever.”
text.KJV: Blessed art thou in the firmament of heaven: and above all to be praised and glorified for ever.
text.NABRE: Blessed are you in the firmament of heaven, praiseworthy and glorious forever.
text.DRB: Blessed art thou in the firmament of heaven: and laudable and glorious for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, all you works of the Lord; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O all ye works of the Lord, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever,
text.NABRE: Bless the Lord, all you works of the Lord, praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: All works of our Lord bless ye our Lord, praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, you heavens; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye heavens, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Angels of the Lord, bless the Lord, praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Bless our Lord ye Angels of our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, you angels of the Lord; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye angels of the Lord, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: You heavens, bless the Lord, praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Ye heavens bless our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, all you waters above the heavens; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O all ye waters that be above the heaven, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: All you waters above the heavens, bless the Lord, praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: All waters that are above the heavens, bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, all you powers of the Lord; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O all ye powers of the Lord, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: All you powers, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Bless our Lord all ye powers of our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, sun and moon; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye sun and moon, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Sun and moon, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Sun and moon bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, stars of heaven; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye stars of heaven, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Stars of heaven, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Stars of heavens bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, all rain and dew; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O every shower and dew, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Every shower and dew, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Every shower, & dew bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, all you winds; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O all ye winds, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever,
text.NABRE: All you winds, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: All spirits of God bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, fire and heat; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye fire and heat, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Fire and heat, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Fire and heat bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, winter cold and summer heat; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye winter and summer, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Cold and chill, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Cold and heat bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, dews and falling snow; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye dews and storms of snow, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Dew and rain, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Dews and hoar frost bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, nights and days; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye nights and days, bless ye the Lord: bless and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Frost and chill, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Frost and cold bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, light and darkness; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye light and darkness, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Hoarfrost and snow, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Ice and snows bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, ice and cold; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye ice and cold, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Nights and days, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Nights and days bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, frosts and snows; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye frost and snow, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Light and darkness, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Light and darkness bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, lightnings and clouds; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye lightnings and clouds, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Lightnings and clouds, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Lightnings and clouds bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Let the earth bless the Lord; let it sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O let the earth bless the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Let the earth bless the Lord, praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Let the earth bless our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, mountains and hills; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye mountains and little hills, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Mountains and hills, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Mountains and little hills bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, all that grows in the ground; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O all ye things that grow in the earth, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Everything growing on earth, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: All things that spring in the earth bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, you springs; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye mountains, bless ye the Lord: Praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: You springs, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Bless our Lord ye fountains: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, seas and rivers; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye seas and rivers, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Seas and rivers, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Seas and rivers bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, you whales and all that swim in the waters; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye whales, and all that move in the waters, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: You sea monsters and all water creatures, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Whales, and all things that move in the waters, bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, all birds of the air; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O all ye fowls of the air, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: All you birds of the air, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Bless our Lord all ye fowls of heaven: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, all wild animals and cattle; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O all ye beasts and cattle, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: All you beasts, wild and tame, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: All beasts and cattle bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, all people on earth; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye children of men, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: All you mortals, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Sons of men bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, O Israel; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O Israel, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: O Israel, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Let Israel bless our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, you priests of the Lord; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye priests of the Lord, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Priests of the Lord, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Priests of our Lord bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, you servants of the Lord; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye servants of the Lord, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Servants of the Lord, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Servants of our Lord bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, spirits and souls of the righteous; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye spirits and souls of the righteous, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Spirits and souls of the just, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Spirits and souls of the just bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, you who are holy and humble in heart; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever.”
text.KJV: O ye holy and humble men of heart, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
text.NABRE: Holy and humble of heart, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.
text.DRB: Holy and humble of heart bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever.

text.NRSV: “Bless the Lord, Hananiah, Azariah, and Mishael; sing praise to him and highly exalt him forever. For he has rescued us from Hades and saved us from the power of death, and delivered us from the midst of the burning fiery furnace; from the midst of the fire he has delivered us.”
text.KJV: O Ananias, Azarias, and Misael, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever: far he hath delivered us from hell, and saved us from the hand of death, and delivered us out of the midst of the furnace and burning flame: even out of the midst of the fire hath he delivered us.
text.NABRE: Hananiah, Azariah, Mishael, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever. For he has delivered us from Sheol, and saved us from the power of death; He has freed us from the raging flame and delivered us from the fire.
text.DRB: Ananias, Azarias, and Misael, bless ye our Lord: praise and superexalt him for ever. Because he hath delivered us from hell, and saved us out of the hand of death, and delivered us out of the midst of the burning flame, and out of the midst of the fire hath he rid us.

text.NRSV: “Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.”
text.KJV: O give thanks unto the Lord, because he is gracious: for his mercy endureth for ever.
text.NABRE: Give thanks to the Lord, who is good, whose mercy endures forever.
text.DRB: Confess ye to our Lord, because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.

text.NRSV: “All who worship the Lord, bless the God of gods, sing praise to him and give thanks to him, for his mercy endures forever.”
text.KJV: O all ye that worship the Lord, bless the God of gods, praise him, and give him thanks: for his mercy endureth for ever.
text.NABRE: Bless the God of gods, all you who fear the Lord; praise and give thanks, for his mercy endures forever.”
text.DRB: All religious bless ye our Lord the God of gods: praise and confess ye to him, because his mercy is unto all worlds.

text.NRSV: Then King Nebuchadnezzar was astonished and rose up quickly. He said to his counselors, “Was it not three men that we threw bound into the fire?” They answered the king, “True, O king.”
text.KJV: Then Nebuchadnezzar the king was astonied, and rose up in haste, and spake, and said unto his counsellors, Did not we cast three men bound into the midst of the fire? They answered and said unto the king, True, O king.

text.NRSV: He replied, “But I see four men unbound, walking in the middle of the fire, and they are not hurt; and the fourth has the appearance of a god.”
text.KJV: He answered and said, Lo, I see four men loose, walking in the midst of the fire, and they have no hurt; and the form of the fourth is like the Son of God.

text.NRSV: Nebuchadnezzar then approached the door of the furnace of blazing fire and said, “Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, servants of the Most High God, come out! Come here!” So Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego came out from the fire.
text.KJV: Then Nebuchadnezzar came near to the mouth of the burning fiery furnace, and spake, and said, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, ye servants of the most high God, come forth, and come hither. Then Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, came forth of the midst of the fire.

text.NRSV: And the satraps, the prefects, the governors, and the king’s counselors gathered together and saw that the fire had not had any power over the bodies of those men; the hair of their heads was not singed, their tunics were not harmed, and not even the smell of fire came from them.
text.KJV: And the princes, governors, and captains, and the king’s counsellors, being gathered together, saw these men, upon whose bodies the fire had no power, nor was an hair of their head singed, neither were their coats changed, nor the smell of fire had passed on them.

text.NRSV: Nebuchadnezzar said, “Blessed be the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, who has sent his angel and delivered his servants who trusted in him. They disobeyed the king’s command and yielded up their bodies rather than serve and worship any god except their own God.
text.KJV: Then Nebuchadnezzar spake, and said, Blessed be the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, who hath sent his angel, and delivered his servants that trusted in him, and have changed the king’s word, and yielded their bodies, that they might not serve nor worship any god, except their own God.

text.NRSV: Therefore I make a decree: Any people, nation, or language that utters blasphemy against the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego shall be torn limb from limb, and their houses laid in ruins; for there is no other god who is able to deliver in this way.”
text.KJV: Therefore I make a decree, That every people, nation, and language, which speak any thing amiss against the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, shall be cut in pieces, and their houses shall be made a dunghill: because there is no other God that can deliver after this sort.

text.NRSV: Then the king promoted Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego in the province of Babylon.
text.KJV: Then the king promoted Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, in the province of Babylon.

text.NRSV: King Nebuchadnezzar to all peoples, nations, and languages that live throughout the whole world: May you have abundant prosperity!
text.KJV: Nebuchadnezzar the king, unto all people, nations, and languages, that dwell in all the earth; Peace be multiplied unto you.

text.NRSV: The signs and wonders that the Most High God has worked for me I am pleased to recount.
text.KJV: I thought it good to shew the signs and wonders that the high God hath wrought toward me.

text.NRSV: How great are his signs, how mighty his wonders! His kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and his sovereignty endures from generation to generation.
text.KJV: How great are his signs! and how mighty are his wonders! his kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and his dominion is from generation to generation.

text.NRSV: I, Nebuchadnezzar, was living at ease in my home and prospering in my palace.
text.KJV: I Nebuchadnezzar was at rest in mine house, and flourishing in my palace:

text.NRSV: I saw a dream that frightened me; my thoughts in bed and the visions of my head terrified me.
text.KJV: I saw a dream which made me afraid, and the thoughts upon my bed and the visions of my head troubled me.

text.NRSV: So I made a decree that all the wise men of Babylon should be brought before me, in order that they might tell me the interpretation of the dream.
text.KJV: Therefore made I a decree to bring in all the wise men of Babylon before me, that they might make known unto me the interpretation of the dream.

text.NRSV: When the magicians, the enchanters, the Chaldeans, and the diviners came in, I told them the dream, but they could not tell me its interpretation.
text.KJV: Then came in the magicians, the astrologers, the Chaldeans, and the soothsayers: and I told the dream before them; but they did not make known unto me the interpretation thereof.

text.NRSV: At last Daniel came in before me—he who was named Belteshazzar after the name of my god, and who is endowed with a spirit of the holy gods—and I told him the dream:
text.KJV: But at the last Daniel came in before me, whose name was Belteshazzar, according to the name of my god, and in whom is the spirit of the holy gods: and before him I told the dream, saying,

text.NRSV: “O Belteshazzar, chief of the magicians, I know that you are endowed with a spirit of the holy gods and that no mystery is too difficult for you. Hear the visions of my dream that I saw and tell me its interpretation.
text.KJV: O Belteshazzar, master of the magicians, because I know that the spirit of the holy gods is in thee, and no secret troubleth thee, tell me the visions of my dream that I have seen, and the interpretation thereof.

text.NRSV: Upon my bed this is what I saw; there was a tree at the center of the earth, and its height was great.
text.KJV: Thus were the visions of mine head in my bed; I saw, and behold a tree in the midst of the earth, and the height thereof was great.

text.NRSV: The tree grew and became strong; its top reached to heaven, and it was visible to the ends of the whole earth.
text.KJV: The tree grew, and was strong, and the height thereof reached unto heaven, and the sight thereof to the end of all the earth:

text.NRSV: Its foliage was beautiful and its fruit abundant, and it provided food for all. The animals of the field found shade under it, the birds of the air nested in its branches, and from it all living beings were fed.
text.KJV: The leaves thereof were fair, and the fruit thereof much, and in it was meat for all: the beasts of the field had shadow under it, and the fowls of the heaven dwelt in the boughs thereof, and all flesh was fed of it.

text.NRSV: I continued to look in the visions of my head as I lay in bed, and there was a holy watcher, coming down from heaven.
text.KJV: I saw in the visions of my head upon my bed, and, behold, a watcher and an holy one came down from heaven;

text.NRSV: He cried aloud and said: ‘Cut down the tree and chop off its branches, strip off its foliage and scatter its fruit. Let the animals flee from under it and the birds from its branches.
text.KJV: He cried aloud, and said thus, Hew down the tree, and cut off his branches, shake off his leaves, and scatter his fruit: let the beasts get away from under it, and the fowls from his branches:

text.NRSV: But leave its stump and roots in the ground, with a band of iron and bronze, in the tender grass of the field. Let him be bathed with the dew of heaven, and let his lot be with the animals of the field in the grass of the earth.
text.KJV: Nevertheless leave the stump of his roots in the earth, even with a band of iron and brass, in the tender grass of the field; and let it be wet with the dew of heaven, and let his portion be with the beasts in the grass of the earth:

text.NRSV: Let his mind be changed from that of a human, and let the mind of an animal be given to him. And let seven times pass over him.
text.KJV: Let his heart be changed from man’s, and let a beast’s heart be given unto him; and let seven times pass over him.

text.NRSV: The sentence is rendered by decree of the watchers, the decision is given by word of the holy ones, in order that all who live may know that the Most High is sovereign over the kingdom of mortals; he gives it to whom he will and sets over it the lowliest of human beings.’
text.KJV: This matter is by the decree of the watchers, and the demand by the word of the holy ones: to the intent that the living may know that the most High ruleth in the kingdom of men, and giveth it to whomsoever he will, and setteth up over it the basest of men.

text.NRSV: “This is the dream that I, King Nebuchadnezzar, saw. Now you, Belteshazzar, tell me the interpretation, since all the wise men of my kingdom are unable to tell me the interpretation; you are able, however, for you are endowed with a spirit of the holy gods.”
text.KJV: This dream I king Nebuchadnezzar have seen. Now thou, O Belteshazzar, declare the interpretation thereof, forasmuch as all the wise men of my kingdom are not able to make known unto me the interpretation: but thou art able; for the spirit of the holy gods is in thee.

text.NRSV: Then Daniel, who was called Belteshazzar, was severely distressed for a while, and his thoughts terrified him. The king said, “Belteshazzar, do not let the dream or the interpretation terrify you.” Belteshazzar answered, “My lord, may the dream be for those who hate you, and its interpretation for your enemies!
text.KJV: Then Daniel, whose name was Belteshazzar, was astonied for one hour, and his thoughts troubled him. The king spake, and said, Belteshazzar, let not the dream, or the interpretation thereof, trouble thee. Belteshazzar answered and said, My lord, the dream be to them that hate thee, and the interpretation thereof to thine enemies.

text.NRSV: The tree that you saw, which grew and became strong, so that its top reached to heaven and was visible to the end of the whole earth,
text.KJV: The tree that thou sawest, which grew, and was strong, whose height reached unto the heaven, and the sight thereof to all the earth;

text.NRSV: whose foliage was beautiful and its fruit abundant, and which provided food for all, under which animals of the field lived, and in whose branches the birds of the air had nests—
text.KJV: Whose leaves were fair, and the fruit thereof much, and in it was meat for all; under which the beasts of the field dwelt, and upon whose branches the fowls of the heaven had their habitation:

text.NRSV: it is you, O king! You have grown and become strong. Your greatness has increased and reaches to heaven, and your sovereignty to the ends of the earth.
text.KJV: It is thou, O king, that art grown and become strong: for thy greatness is grown, and reacheth unto heaven, and thy dominion to the end of the earth.

text.NRSV: And whereas the king saw a holy watcher coming down from heaven and saying, ‘Cut down the tree and destroy it, but leave its stump and roots in the ground, with a band of iron and bronze, in the tender grass of the field; and let him be bathed with the dew of heaven, and let his lot be with the animals of the field, until seven times pass over him’—
text.KJV: And whereas the king saw a watcher and an holy one coming down from heaven, and saying, Hew the tree down, and destroy it; yet leave the stump of the roots thereof in the earth, even with a band of iron and brass, in the tender grass of the field; and let it be wet with the dew of heaven, and let his portion be with the beasts of the field, till seven times pass over him;

text.NRSV: this is the interpretation, O king: it is a decree of the Most High that has come upon my lord the king:
text.KJV: This is the interpretation, O king, and this is the decree of the most High, which is come upon my lord the king:

text.NRSV: you shall be driven away from human society, and your dwelling shall be with the wild animals. You shall be made to eat grass like oxen, and you shall be bathed with the dew of heaven, and seven times shall pass over you, until you have learned that the Most High has sovereignty over the kingdom of mortals and gives it to whom he will.
text.KJV: That they shall drive thee from men, and thy dwelling shall be with the beasts of the field, and they shall make thee to eat grass as oxen, and they shall wet thee with the dew of heaven, and seven times shall pass over thee, till thou know that the most High ruleth in the kingdom of men, and giveth it to whomsoever he will.

text.NRSV: As it was commanded to leave the stump and roots of the tree, your kingdom shall be re-established for you from the time that you learn that Heaven is sovereign.
text.KJV: And whereas they commanded to leave the stump of the tree roots; thy kingdom shall be sure unto thee, after that thou shalt have known that the heavens do rule.

text.NRSV: Therefore, O king, may my counsel be acceptable to you: atone for your sins with righteousness, and your iniquities with mercy to the oppressed, so that your prosperity may perhaps be prolonged.”
text.KJV: Wherefore, O king, let my counsel be acceptable unto thee, and break off thy sins by righteousness, and thine iniquities by shewing mercy to the poor; if it may be a lengthening of thy tranquillity.

text.NRSV: All this came upon King Nebuchadnezzar.
text.KJV: All this came upon the king Nebuchadnezzar.

text.NRSV: At the end of twelve months he was walking on the roof of the royal palace of Babylon,
text.KJV: At the end of twelve months he walked in the palace of the kingdom of Babylon.

text.NRSV: and the king uttered these words: “Is this not magnificent Babylon, which I have built as a royal residence by my mighty power and for my glorious majesty?”
text.KJV: The king spake, and said, Is not this great Babylon, that I have built for the house of the kingdom by the might of my power, and for the honour of my majesty?

text.NRSV: While the words were still in the king’s mouth, a voice came from heaven: “O King Nebuchadnezzar, to you it is declared: The kingdom has departed from you!
text.KJV: While the word was in the king’s mouth, there fell a voice from heaven, saying, O king Nebuchadnezzar, to thee it is spoken; The kingdom is departed from thee.

text.NRSV: You shall be driven away from human society, and your dwelling shall be with the wild animals. You shall be made to eat grass like oxen, and seven times shall pass over you, until you have learned that the Most High has sovereignty over the kingdom of mortals and gives it to whom he will.”
text.KJV: And they shall drive thee from men, and thy dwelling shall be with the beasts of the field: they shall make thee to eat grass as oxen, and seven times shall pass over thee, until thou know that the most High ruleth in the kingdom of men, and giveth it to whomsoever he will.

text.NRSV: Immediately the sentence was fulfilled against Nebuchadnezzar. He was driven away from human society, ate grass like oxen, and his body was bathed with the dew of heaven, until his hair grew as long as eagles’ feathers and his nails became like birds’ claws.
text.KJV: The same hour was the thing fulfilled upon Nebuchadnezzar: and he was driven from men, and did eat grass as oxen, and his body was wet with the dew of heaven, till his hairs were grown like eagles’ feathers, and his nails like birds’ claws.

text.NRSV: When that period was over, I, Nebuchadnezzar, lifted my eyes to heaven, and my reason returned to me. I blessed the Most High, and praised and honored the one who lives forever. For his sovereignty is an everlasting sovereignty, and his kingdom endures from generation to generation.
text.KJV: And at the end of the days I Nebuchadnezzar lifted up mine eyes unto heaven, and mine understanding returned unto me, and I blessed the most High, and I praised and honoured him that liveth for ever, whose dominion is an everlasting dominion, and his kingdom is from generation to generation:

text.NRSV: All the inhabitants of the earth are accounted as nothing, and he does what he wills with the host of heaven and the inhabitants of the earth. There is no one who can stay his hand or say to him, “What are you doing?”
text.KJV: And all the inhabitants of the earth are reputed as nothing: and he doeth according to his will in the army of heaven, and among the inhabitants of the earth: and none can stay his hand, or say unto him, What doest thou?

text.NRSV: At that time my reason returned to me; and my majesty and splendor were restored to me for the glory of my kingdom. My counselors and my lords sought me out, I was re-established in my kingdom, and still more greatness was added to me.
text.KJV: At the same time my reason returned unto me; and for the glory of my kingdom, mine honour and brightness returned unto me; and my counsellors and my lords sought unto me; and I was established in my kingdom, and excellent majesty was added unto me.

text.NRSV: Now I, Nebuchadnezzar, praise and extol and honor the King of heaven, for all his works are truth, and his ways are justice; and he is able to humble those who walk in pride.
text.KJV: Now I Nebuchadnezzar praise and extol and honour the King of heaven, all whose works are truth, and his ways judgment: and those that walk in pride he is able to abase.

text.NRSV: King Belshazzar made a great festival for a thousand of his lords, and he was drinking wine in the presence of the thousand.
text.KJV: Belshazzar the king made a great feast to a thousand of his lords, and drank wine before the thousand.

text.NRSV: Under the influence of the wine, Belshazzar commanded that they bring in the vessels of gold and silver that his father Nebuchadnezzar had taken out of the temple in Jerusalem, so that the king and his lords, his wives, and his concubines might drink from them.
